_metadata.match: Houston Dynamo FC vs San Diego FC

new.name: Reese Miller
new.note: Added to injury report today. Rookie depth defender.

good_news.name: Artur
good_news.note: Dynamo captain Artur returned to the pitch as a 2nd-half sub in the Orlando win (Apr 18), completing his comeback from the preseason knee injury. Available tonight as depth.
good_news.source: Houston Dynamo FC official match preview, Apr 21 2026

mcvey_starting_confirmed.note: McVey back from suspension — got his 3rd red card of 2026 vs Minnesota (4/11). East Village Times: 'McVey's return to the midfield will be important to San Diego's chance to bounce back.' Walking disciplinary nightmare. 6 red cards as a team in 2026 already (vs 4 all of last year).
mcvey_starting_confirmed.coach_quote: Mikey Varas: 'I can't explain what's happening... I think we're not receiving some calls.' Tverskov bluntly: 'It needs to stop.'

san_diego_gameday.winless_streak_updated: SIX matches without a win (L-L-D-L-L-L), including 4-2 loss at RSL Saturday. Went from 3-0-2 undefeated start to 3-3-2 in collapse mode.

sargeant_subplot.headline: The one that got away (for a draft pick)
sargeant_subplot.detail: San Diego has Kieran Sargeant — a former Dynamo Academy Homegrown Player. Houston literally traded his right of first refusal to SDFC for a 2026 third-round SuperDraft pick. He's currently injured, won't feature tonight, but: imagine giving away a homegrown defender when your own backline is held together with Scotch tape and prayer.
sargeant_subplot.source: Houston Dynamo FC official match preview

dreyer_hot_streak.update: Scored in both matches vs RSL this month. Converted a penalty Saturday. Now at 4G/3A in 8 matches. Named 2025 MLS Newcomer of the Year — not just MVP runner-up, he also won the shiny participation trophy.
dreyer_hot_streak.wiki_tea: Danish, 27, signed to SDFC in January 2025 on a 3-year deal. Scored San Diego's first-ever goal in club history. Two of his Danish countrymen — Tverskov and Ingvartsen — were already there, so he joined the Denmark colony.
dreyer_hot_streak.girlfriend_guide_angle: If you're picking ONE opposition player to scream about, it's him. Has the bio of a business major ('For inquiries') and the goals of a man who knows he's the prettiest one on the pitch.

ingvartsen_quiet_dangerous.stat: Team-leading 5 goals, 2 assists in 2026 (7 contributions, 8 matches). Tied with Guilherme on goal count.
ingvartsen_quiet_dangerous.note: The 'Mindset | Performance | Business' bro is actually producing. Don't let the LinkedIn bio fool you.

tverskov_frustrated_captain.detail: Captain (C), playing every single minute of the season (675 min in 8 matches), and publicly said of the red card epidemic: 'It needs to stop.' Starting midfielder for a Denmark rotation spot. Still only 6.6K on IG. The captain with no brand.

matchup_significance.houston_opportunity: SDFC rolls into Shell Energy on a 6-game winless streak with 9 players out and a ticking-red-card time bomb at LB. Houston are home favorites (+151 moneyline was UNDERDOG price — SDFC +350 underdogs). Opta: Houston 41%.
matchup_significance.last_meeting_history: When these teams last met at Shell Energy (Oct 4, 2025), SDFC won 4-2 behind a Dreyer brace including an 89th-minute winner. Revenge game energy — though this is a very different SDFC team.
